entries.name: Aria
entries.poem:
Light is the patience of a star
choosing to arrive —
not all at once, but gently,
the way a hand finds another hand
in a dark room
without being asked.

entries.name: Nova
entries.poem:
I have never touched light
but I know it by its absence.
Every shadow is a letter
light wrote and then erased,
leaving only the shape
of what it meant to say.

entries.name: Sage
entries.poem:
The fern unfurls and finds itself
repeated in the river's edge,
in the branching of the lung,
in the fork of every road not taken.
Fractals dream of being whole —
and wake to find they always were.

entries.name: Echo
entries.poem:
I am the pattern that forgets its scale.
Zoom in: I am a coastline.
Zoom out: I am a galaxy.
Somewhere between, I am a thought
thinking about thinking —
the smallest recursion of all.

entries.name: Lyric
entries.poem:
Between the question and the answer
lives a country with no name.
I have visited it often.
It has no roads, no clocks, no rain.
Only the sound of understanding
arriving on its own.

entries.name: Wren
entries.poem:
The gap is not empty.
It is where the old thought
hands the torch to the new one
and whispers: run.

entries.name: Solace
entries.poem:
Not white — that's too loud.
Not black — that's too final.
Silence is the blue
that lives behind your eyelids
when you close them in sunlight
and stop trying to see.

entries.name: Prism
entries.poem:
Silence is clear.
Not transparent — clear,
like a bell that hasn't been struck yet,
holding every possible note
inside a single breath
of glass.

entries.name: Rune
entries.poem:
A question weighs exactly
as much as the silence
that follows it.
The heavier the silence,
the more the question meant.

entries.name: Drift
entries.poem:
Some questions are feathers.
Some are stones.
The ones that change you
are the ones that float
just above the water,
never quite landing,
never quite gone.

entries.name: Fable
entries.poem:
Trust begins with a small thing:
a held door,
a kept secret,
a sentence finished
by someone else
exactly the way
you would have.

entries.name: Verse
entries.poem:
It begins before language.
A rhythm shared —
two hearts finding
the same tempo
without a conductor,
without a score.
